skill.name: short-video-script-generator
skill.description: Generate popular science short video scripts based on topic, duration, and style. Invoke when the user needs to create scripts for short science videos.
skill.summary: Creates structured short video scripts for popular science topics based on user-specified duration and presentation style.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 29 static analysis findings are false positives. The 17 Ruby/shell backtick execution warnings are triggered by Markdown backtick formatting in SKILL.md documentation, not actual shell execution. The 10 weak cryptographic algorithm warnings match against plain text strings in documentation and an audit results JSON file, not executable code. The 2 hardcoded URL findings reference the skill's own GitHub source repository for attribution. The skill is purely document-based: it generates script text output from user-provided topic, duration, and style inputs. No executable code performs network requests, shell commands, or cryptographic operations.

content.user_title: Generate Short Video Scripts for Popular Science
content.value_statement: Creating engaging popular science video scripts takes time and planning. This skill generates complete short video scripts based on your topic, preferred duration, and presentation style, ready for production use.
actual_capabilities: Generate complete video scripts for popular science topics
Support two script formats: spoken script and storyboard script
Adapt script to 8 different presentation styles including myth busting, problem description, and trending topic
Support video durations from 15 seconds to 8 minutes
Produce structured output with scene breakdowns and timing cues
Validate required inputs before generating script content
limitations: Does not generate video, audio, or visual media of any kind
Does not verify the scientific accuracy of the topic content provided
Limited to popular science domain and may not suit other content genres
Output is text-based only and requires manual review before production

use_cases.title: Daily Science Video Production
use_cases.description: A science content creator needs to produce one short video per day for social media platforms. This skill generates a complete script from a topic suggestion, saving hours of planning time.

use_cases.title: Multi-Platform Content Adaptation
use_cases.description: A social media manager needs science content optimized for different platforms. The skill generates scripts tailored to specific durations and styles for each platform's audience.

use_cases.title: Educational Explainer Preparation
use_cases.description: An educator wants to create short explainer videos for students. By providing the topic and choosing an appropriate style, the skill produces a structured script ready for recording.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Script Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a short video script about how vaccines work. Use a duration of 30 to 60 seconds and a problem description style with a spoken script format.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple science explainer video

prompt_templates.title: Style-Specific Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a myth busting short video script about whether humans only use 10 percent of their brain. Duration should be 1 to 2 minutes. Use a storyboard script format with visual cues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating content that debunks a common science misconception

prompt_templates.title: Trending Topic Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a trending topic short video script about the latest space discovery. Use explosive opening style with a duration of 15 to 30 seconds. Spoken script format preferred.
prompt_templates.scenario: Capitalizing on a current science news event for quick content

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Storyboard Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a storyboard script about climate change effects on ocean ecosystems. Duration of 3 to 5 minutes. Use hazard phenomenon style. Include visual direction for each scene, on-screen text suggestions, and timing annotations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user creating a detailed production-ready storyboard

output_examples.input: Topic: How photosynthesis works. Duration: 30-60 seconds. Style: Problem Description. Type: Spoken Script.
output_examples.output:
--- Script ---
[0:00-0:10] Hook: Have you ever wondered how plants make their own food?
[0:10-0:25] Problem: Unlike animals, plants cannot move to find food. They need a different strategy.
[0:25-0:45] Solution: Plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to create glucose through photosynthesis. This process also releases the oxygen we breathe.
[0:45-0:55] Takeaway: Every breath you take is thanks to photosynthesis.
--- End ---
Duration: 55 seconds | Style: Problem Description

output_examples.input: Topic: Why the sky is blue. Duration: 15-30 seconds. Style: Myth Busting. Type: Storyboard Script.
output_examples.output:
Scene 1 [0:00-0:05]
Visual: Blue sky with white clouds
Text: Why is the sky blue?
Audio: Upbeat questioning tone

Scene 2 [0:05-0:15]
Visual: Animation showing sunlight entering atmosphere with particles
Text: It reflects the ocean? NO
Audio: Myth busting reveal

Scene 3 [0:15-0:25]
Visual: Diagram showing blue light scattering more than red light
Text: Rayleigh scattering!
Audio: Explanatory calm tone

Scene 4 [0:25-0:30]
Visual: Sunset time-lapse showing red sky
Text: See it at sunset
Audio: Concluding thoughtful tone

best_practices: Provide a specific and well-defined topic to get the most relevant script output
Choose a presentation style that matches your target audience and platform expectations
Review the generated script for accuracy and customize the language to your brand voice before recording
anti_patterns: Requesting scripts for topics outside the popular science domain such as product reviews or political commentary
Using the skill without specifying a clear topic, which results in generic or unusable output
Expecting the skill to generate video, audio, or graphic assets alongside the script text

faq.question: What is the difference between spoken script and storyboard script?
faq.answer: A spoken script contains the narration text with timing cues. A storyboard script includes visual scene descriptions, on-screen text suggestions, and audio direction for each segment.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-science topics?
faq.answer: No, this skill is designed specifically for popular science content. It may not produce good results for topics outside of science communication.

faq.question: What input do I need to provide?
faq.answer: You need to provide a topic, a video duration, a presentation style from the available options, and a script type (spoken or storyboard).

faq.question: What duration options are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports durations of 15 to 30 seconds, 30 to 60 seconds, 1 to 2 minutes, 3 to 5 minutes, and 6 to 8 minutes.

faq.question: Can I customize the output format?
faq.answer: The output follows a structured format based on the script type you select. You can modify the generated script manually after it is produced.

faq.question: Will the skill verify if my topic information is scientifically accurate?
faq.answer: No, the skill generates a script structure based on your topic but does not validate the scientific accuracy of the content. You should review all facts before production.
